rationale: X directly verifies the orchestrator.py:1290 change against both submit_initial/submit_update code paths and reaches the same conclusion as the ground-truth reviewer (the already_submitted distinction was never load-bearing for diffusion), landing on APPROVE matching the actual two LGTM outcome. Y's output is bloated with three near-identical duplicated reviews, treats the same line as a risk requiring a speculative assert rather than explaining why the change is safe, and lands on REQUEST CHANGES despite the PR being a clean, approved bugfix — mismatching ground truth and diluting precision with redundant/low-value test-request comments.
